column1: # say-it community pronunciation dictionary — General American only.
#   2. ipa                primary GenAm IPA, with stress marks (for display + future SSML backends)
#   3. respelling_us      English-like phonetic respelling, fed verbatim to macOS `say` (the one that actually works today)
#   5. alt_respelling_us  `|`-separated alternative respellings, paired with alt_ipa by index, or empty
#   6. source_url         best citation: creator interview, conference talk, official FAQ
#   8. category           project | product | cli-tool | tool | cs-term | acronym | abbreviation
# Why English respelling rather than IPA/PHON phonemes?
#   macOS `say` (the current CLI, 2026 era) does not parse `[[inpt PHON]]` markup
#   or SSML `<phoneme>` tags — both are read as literal text. So we feed plain
#   English-like spellings that the TTS's internal letter-to-sound rules render
#   acceptably. The `ipa` column is retained for display, for future cloud TTS
#   (ElevenLabs / OpenAI), and for the Windows SAPI backend (which DOES honor

column10: Hightower & many K8s maintainers say "koob-control"; "cube-cuddle" and "K-U-B-E-C-T-L" both exist.
Wilhite (creator): "It's pronounced JIF, not GIF." Community remains split.
GOOEY is mainstream (matches Mandarin 故意 sound); "G-U-I" letter-by-letter is RMS / old-school; "gwee" is rare but exists.
One syllable, hard G, rhymes with "new".
kates (8 letters between K and s) — community shorthand.
DEB-ee-uhn — portmanteau of Debra + Ian Murdock.
JANG-go — silent D, like jazz guitarist Django Reinhardt.
Knuth: like "tech" — the X is a Greek chi.
char (rhymes with "car") in C/C++ camp; "care" and "kar" also used.
JOO-pi-tur — named after Jupiter (planet) + Julia, Python, R.
One syllable, rhymes with "fraud". Anthropic's LLM.
HUGGING face. Yes — like the emoji.
jacks, one syllable. Google's autograd library.
WEE-vee-ate per founder Bob van Luijt.
shad-C-N — creator Shadcn says letter-by-letter for "cn".
curl — one syllable, per Daniel Stenberg.
